name: Chocolate Pancakes with Chocolate-Raspberry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-sized bowl, stir together flour, sugar, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt.
2. In a small bowl, whisk together egg white, milk, oil, and vanilla extract.
3. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and gradually whisk in the liquid mixture until combined. Let the batter stand for 5 minutes or refrigerate for up to 1 hour.
4. Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium low heat. When hot, coat it lightly with non-stick cooking spray.
5. Drop about 1 1/2 tablespoons of batter per pancake into the pan and cook until the underside is browned and the bubbles on top remain open, about 1 to 1 1/2 minutes.
6. Turn the pancakes over and cook until the underside is browned, about 15 seconds.
7. Repeat with the remaining batter.
8. Arrange the pancakes on dessert plates. Top with frozen yogurt and Chocolate-Raspberry Sauce. Garnish with raspberries. Serves 2.
9. Chocolate-Raspberry Sauce:
a. In a small saucepan, whisk together sugar, cocoa powder, arrowroot or cornstarch.
b. Gradually whisk in water (1/4 cup) and raspberry jam.
c. Bring to a simmer over medium heat, whisking constantly.
d. Remove from the heat and stir in framboise if using.
e. Let cool slightly. Makes 2/3 cup.
